title: Microsoft Entra Application
description: Schema for a Microsoft Entra ID application registration as represented in the Microsoft Graph API. Defines the application's identity configuration, credentials, permissions, redirect URIs, and sign-in settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the application object (GUID). This is the directory object ID, not the appId/client ID.

appId.description: The unique application (client) identifier assigned by Microsoft Entra ID during app registration.

displayName.description: The display name for the application.

description.description: Free text field to provide a description of the application object to end users.

signInAudience.description: Specifies which Microsoft accounts are supported for the current application.

identifierUris.description: User-defined URIs that uniquely identify a Web application within its Microsoft Entra tenant or verified custom domain (e.g., api://contoso.com/myapp).

requiredResourceAccess.description: Specifies the resources that the application needs access to and the set of OAuth permission scopes and app roles required under each resource.

appRoles.description: Collection of roles defined for the application. These roles can be assigned to users, groups, or service principals.

keyCredentials.description: Collection of key (certificate) credentials associated with the application for token signing and verification.

passwordCredentials.description: Collection of password credentials (client secrets) associated with the application.

tags.description: Custom strings that can be used to categorize and identify the application.

groupMembershipClaims.description: Configures the groups claim issued in user or OAuth 2.0 access tokens.

isFallbackPublicClient.description: Specifies the fallback application type as public client (e.g., installed application on a mobile device). Default is false.

defaultRedirectUri.description: The default redirect URI. If specified, it is used when no specific redirect URI is matched.

certification.description: Publisher certification status of the application.

isPublisherAttested.description: Whether the application has been attested by the publisher.

isCertifiedByMicrosoft.description: Whether the application has been certified by Microsoft.

publisherDomain.description: The verified publisher domain for the application.

createdDateTime.description: The date and time the application was registered.

deletedDateTime.description: The date and time the application was deleted.

WebApplication.description: Settings for a web application including redirect URIs and implicit grant configuration.

redirectUris.description: Specifies URLs to which Azure AD will redirect after authentication for web applications.

homePageUrl.description: Home page or landing page URL of the application.

logoutUrl.description: URL used by the authorization service to sign out the user using front-channel, back-channel, or SAML logout protocols.

implicitGrantSettings.description: Specifies whether this web application can request tokens using the OAuth 2.0 implicit flow.

enableIdTokenIssuance.description: Specifies whether this web application can request an ID token using the OAuth 2.0 implicit flow.

enableAccessTokenIssuance.description: Specifies whether this web application can request an access token using the OAuth 2.0 implicit flow.

SpaApplication.description: Settings for a single-page application including redirect URIs for MSAL.js 2.0 auth code flow with PKCE.

redirectUris.description: Specifies redirect URIs for the SPA to receive authorization codes and access tokens.

PublicClientApplication.description: Settings for installed (public client) applications on mobile and desktop devices.

redirectUris.description: Specifies redirect URIs for native/public client applications (mobile and desktop).

ApiApplication.description: Settings for an application that implements a web API including permission scopes and pre-authorized applications.

acceptMappedClaims.description: When true, allows an application to use claims mapping without specifying a custom signing key.

knownClientApplications.description: Client application IDs considered as known clients for bundling consent.

oauth2PermissionScopes.description: The definition of the delegated permissions (OAuth 2.0 scopes) exposed by the web API.

preAuthorizedApplications.description: Lists applications pre-authorized with the specified delegated permissions to access this API without user consent.

appId.description: The appId of the pre-authorized client application.

delegatedPermissionIds.description: The IDs of the OAuth 2.0 permission scopes the client is pre-authorized for.

requestedAccessTokenVersion.description: Specifies the access token version expected by this resource. Values: 1 (v1.0 tokens) or 2 (v2.0 tokens).

PermissionScope.description: Defines a delegated permission (OAuth 2.0 scope) that a web API application exposes to client applications.

id.description: Unique delegated permission identifier inside the collection of scopes.

value.description: The scope value string included in OAuth 2.0 access tokens (e.g., User.Read, Mail.Send).

type.description: Whether this scope can be consented by end users or only by admins.

adminConsentDisplayName.description: Title of the permission shown on the admin consent page.

adminConsentDescription.description: Description of the permission shown on the admin consent page.

userConsentDisplayName.description: Title of the permission shown on the user consent page.

userConsentDescription.description: Description of the permission shown on the user consent page.

isEnabled.description: When creating or updating a permission, this must be set to true. To delete a permission, first set to false.

RequiredResourceAccess.description: Specifies the set of OAuth 2.0 permission scopes and app roles under a specified resource that an application requires.

resourceAppId.description: The appId of the resource application (e.g., 00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000 for Microsoft Graph).

resourceAccess.description: The list of OAuth 2.0 permission scopes and app roles required from the specified resource.

id.description: The unique identifier for an app role or OAuth2 permission scope exposed by the resource application.

type.description: Specifies whether the id references a delegated permission (Scope) or an application permission (Role).

AppRole.description: Represents an application role that can be requested by a client application calling another app, or assigned to users or groups.

id.description: Unique role identifier inside the appRoles collection.

allowedMemberTypes.description: Specifies whether the app role can be assigned to users/groups, applications, or both.

displayName.description: Display name for the permission that appears in the app role assignment and consent experiences.

description.description: Description of the permission that appears in admin app assignment and consent experiences.

value.description: Value included in the roles claim in ID tokens and access tokens authenticating an assigned user or service principal.

isEnabled.description: When creating or updating an app role, this must be set to true.

origin.description: Specifies if the app role is defined on the application or service principal object.

KeyCredential.description: Contains a key (certificate) credential associated with an application used for token signing and verification.

keyId.description: The unique identifier for the key.

usage.description: A string that describes the purpose for which the key can be used.

key.description: The certificate's raw data in byte array converted to Base64 string.

startDateTime.description: The date and time at which the credential becomes valid.

endDateTime.description: The date and time at which the credential expires.

customKeyIdentifier.description: Custom key identifier (typically the certificate thumbprint).

PasswordCredential.description: Represents a password credential (client secret) associated with an application.

keyId.description: The unique identifier for the password.

displayName.description: Friendly name for the password. Maximum length is 32 characters.

hint.description: Contains the first three characters of the password.

secretText.description: The strong password or secret generated by Microsoft Entra ID. Only returned at creation time and cannot be retrieved later.

startDateTime.description: The date and time at which the password becomes valid.

endDateTime.description: The date and time at which the password expires.

OptionalClaims.description: Declares the optional claims requested by an application. The application can configure optional claims to be returned in tokens from the Microsoft identity platform.

idToken.description: The optional claims requested in the JWT ID token.

accessToken.description: The optional claims requested in the JWT access token.

saml2Token.description: The optional claims requested in the SAML token.

OptionalClaim.description: An optional claim associated with an application.

name.description: The name of the optional claim.

source.description: The source (directory object) of the claim. If null, the claim is a predefined optional claim.

essential.description: If true, the claim specified by the client is necessary to ensure a smooth authorization experience.

InformationalUrl.description: Basic profile information of the application for user-facing scenarios.

logoUrl.description: CDN URL to the application's logo.

marketingUrl.description: Link to the application's marketing page.

privacyStatementUrl.description: Link to the application's privacy statement.

supportUrl.description: Link to the application's support page.

termsOfServiceUrl.description: Link to the application's terms of service statement.
